findings.description: Outputs table records tests/loop-escalation.tests.sh sha256 as 37056156032bc180aed0a3ea07babbd6e7b0ffa131c5ee7d00c78a950fff6ee4, but the actual committed/working-tree file is a572dbc91fe821ca7ab9e5a996aa3220612a8dcc72cf7f0cf2b8b1d617aa8b78. This misrecord excluded the core deliverable from the deterministic manifest and required an orchestrator exception. Underlying artifact is correct and its provenance was independently confirmed; only the recorded hash is wrong.
findings.evidence: shasum -a 256 tests/loop-escalation.tests.sh -> a572dbc9...; git show f279536:tests/loop-escalation.tests.sh -> a572dbc9... (byte-identical, empty git diff); report row shows 37056156...

findings.description: TEST-011.8 validates a hand-built blocked-state fixture against jq/PowerShell-transcribed schema constraints rather than loading contracts/terminal-tier-blocked-state.schema.json or validating select-agent-model's real terminal-tier output. Follows the established assert_artifacts_schema convention (no JSON-schema validator in-repo) and asserts the schema file's existence, but schema-file fidelity to the transcription is not independently verifiable within the read boundary, and real-file drift would not be caught by this leg.
findings.evidence: Line-inspected TEST-011.8: hardcoded jq key-set/pattern/enum/range checks on a jq -n fixture; SCHEMA_JSON only existence-checked at lines 83-85; report Specification Differences acknowledges the jq-replicated approach.

findings.description: Spec carries the factually-inaccurate premise that this suite becomes check-terminal-tier-resume.sh's 'first direct driver'. tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh already drives that script directly (positive complete-evidence pass plus unchanged-contract rejection). The implementer correctly surfaced this as a spec defect and recorded the correction instead of silently editing the hash-frozen spec; the suite's genuinely-new coverage (tasks.md-side reapproval deny/permit, TEST-011.10/.11) is real. Recorded as spec drift for the spec owner to reconcile; not an implementation defect.
findings.evidence: grep -n check-terminal-tier-resume tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh -> lines 35,36,68; lines 458/463 run bash/pwsh RESUME drives with positive+negative cases.

checked: Verified manifest self-hash = 821f991e...; all 20 manifest-listed file content hashes recomputed and matched except identity-ledger.json.

Resolved the identity-ledger hash gap: reconstructed the pre-reservation ledger (seq 1..205 + trailing newline) and hashed it to exactly the manifest identity_ledger_sha256; current ledger = that state plus the atomically-appended seq206 record. run_id/host_session_id appear exactly once; chain intact, identity not reused.
Validated the orchestrator .sh exception: current file hash = git show f279536 blob hash = asserted value; then read the current file.
Re-ran bash tests/loop-escalation.tests.sh -> 22 passed, 0 failed, exit 0.
Re-ran pwsh tests/loop-escalation.tests.ps1 -> 21 passed, 0 failed, exit 0.

Line-inspected all 7 mandated items in the .sh (spot-checked the .ps1 twin): cycle-limit 0/1/2/3 with absent dir = 0 driving the real script; T-001-vs-T-0010 prefix collision + substring-grep mutation on a mktemp copy with live negative self-check; select-agent-model next_tier chain via real script; terminal-tier blocked-state structural check (see F-2); check-terminal-tier-resume deny/permit pair on the real script; TEST-012 drives the REAL validate-review-context-set.sh with two genuine negative self-checks; TEST-013 restricted-PATH degradation as named SKIP with a fail() guard.

Confirmed no completion-faking / test-nerfing / real-gate writes: git status clean after running both suites; git diff empty for the driven scripts, the contracts schema, and tests/template-validator-parity.tests.sh (referenced, unedited).
Corroborated OQ-4 correction factuality via grep of tests/agent-model-routing.tests.sh.
Verified registration: tests/run-all.sh:48, tests/run-all.ps1:30, .github/workflows/test.yml:105/109; CHANGELOG.md:85 cites Issue #144; crlf-parity 8 passed, 0 failed.
summary: T-004's loop-escalation suite is genuinely implemented and verified: both lanes run green independently (bash 22/0, pwsh 21/0), all acceptance criteria are exercised by real checks driving the real scripts read-only with authentic negative self-checks and mktemp-scoped fixtures, and no gate/script/contract/parity-suite file is modified. Three non-blocking Minor findings (misrecorded .sh hash with independently-confirmed provenance, convention-based transcribed schema check, correctly-surfaced upstream OQ-4 spec defect). Zero Critical and zero Major: PASS.
